structural_claim: Collective quantum phenomena emerge from many-body correlations creating qualitatively new states that cannot be understood from single-particle physics

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Strongly correlated electrons form incompressible quantum fluids with emergent quasiparticles having fractional statistics
Macroscopic quantum coherence emerges from microscopic pairing in many-body systems
Nuclei exhibit collective oscillation modes demonstrating quantum fluid behavior with collective degrees of freedom
Nucleons lose individual identity in nuclear medium, with quark distributions modified by collective nuclear environment
